mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above 5.5% triggers a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 110 triggers a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 110 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1200 triggers a demand surge that increases corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1200 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial unemployment claims below 250,000 triggers a labor market upturn that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 250,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 11700 triggers a demand surge that increases corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 11700 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 11700 and a decline in industrial production below 110 triggers a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 11700 and INDPRO < 110 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of instances.
